title: Dupatya geniculata
taxonomy: Dupatya geniculata is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in Malaysia and Indonesia. Within the family Annonaceae, Dupatya geniculata is classified under the subfamily Annonoideae. This species belongs to the genus Dupatya, which is part of the order Magnoliales. The classification of Dupatya geniculata is further detailed in the botanical classification system.
uses: Dupatya geniculata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have wound-healing properties, and its extracts have been used to treat skin infections and promote tissue regeneration. In addition, Dupatya geniculata has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Its antiseptic properties also make it effective against fungal and bacterial infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and benefits.
